name: Serialize Both, Then Compare
summary: Flatten each tree to a preorder list (with null markers), then compare the two lists element by element.
visualizationIntent: init: build both trees. serialize: flatten p into sp and q into sq by preorder (each real node emits its value; each missing child emits a null marker ∅) — the two arrays grow. compare: a single cursor i sweeps both arrays in step; equal cells turn visited, and the first differing cell (or a length difference) makes same false. return: show whether the two serializations are identical.

lineExplanations.7: Loop over p's slots to build nodes.
lineExplanations.9: Create this p node with empty child links.
lineExplanations.10: If a slot is left for a left child…
lineExplanations.12: link it as the left child.
lineExplanations.14: If a slot is left for a right child…
lineExplanations.16: link it as the right child.
lineExplanations.24: Loop over q's slots to build nodes.
lineExplanations.26: Create this q node with empty child links.
lineExplanations.27: If a slot is left for a left child…
lineExplanations.29: link it as the left child.
lineExplanations.31: If a slot is left for a right child…
lineExplanations.33: link it as the right child.
lineExplanations.38: A stack drives p's preorder walk.
lineExplanations.39: Seed it with p's root (or None if empty).
lineExplanations.42: If it's empty, emit a null marker.
lineExplanations.46: Push its right child (visited after left).
lineExplanations.49: A stack drives q's preorder walk.
lineExplanations.50: Seed it with q's root (or None if empty).
lineExplanations.53: If it's empty, emit a null marker.
lineExplanations.60: If the two lists differ in length…
lineExplanations.63: Sweep both lists while they still agree.
lineExplanations.64: If the two cells at i differ…
lineExplanations.67: Return whether the two serializations match.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def isSameTree(self, p_values, q_values): the two trees as level-order arrays.
syntaxExplanations.7: for i in range(pn): loops over p's slots.
syntaxExplanations.8: p_values[i] is not None skips nulls.
syntaxExplanations.9: A dict literal with id, value, None children.
syntaxExplanations.10: pc < pn checks a left-child slot exists.
syntaxExplanations.11: p_values[pc] is not None checks it's real.
syntaxExplanations.12: node["left"] = pc records the left child.
syntaxExplanations.14: pc < pn checks a right-child slot exists.
syntaxExplanations.15: p_values[pc] is not None checks it's real.
syntaxExplanations.16: node["right"] = pc records the right child.
syntaxExplanations.19: p_by[i] = node stores it by id.
syntaxExplanations.24: for i in range(qn): loops over q's slots.
syntaxExplanations.25: q_values[i] is not None skips nulls.
syntaxExplanations.26: A dict literal with id, value, None children.
syntaxExplanations.27: qc < qn checks a left-child slot exists.
syntaxExplanations.28: q_values[qc] is not None checks it's real.
syntaxExplanations.29: node["left"] = qc records the left child.
syntaxExplanations.31: qc < qn checks a right-child slot exists.
syntaxExplanations.32: q_values[qc] is not None checks it's real.
syntaxExplanations.33: node["right"] = qc records the right child.
syntaxExplanations.36: q_by[i] = node stores it by id.
syntaxExplanations.39: st.append(0 if pn > 0 else None) seeds the root.
syntaxExplanations.41: nd = st.pop() takes the next position.
syntaxExplanations.42: if nd is None: an empty (missing) position.
syntaxExplanations.44: else: a real node (bare else — no step).
syntaxExplanations.50: st2.append(0 if qn > 0 else None) seeds q's root.
syntaxExplanations.52: nd = st2.pop() takes the next position.
syntaxExplanations.53: if nd is None: an empty position.
syntaxExplanations.55: else: a real node (bare else — no step).
syntaxExplanations.59: same = True is the running answer.
syntaxExplanations.63: while i < len(sp) and same: sweep while equal.
syntaxExplanations.64: if sp[i] != sq[i]: the cells differ.
syntaxExplanations.67: return same hands back the answer.
